title: Earnings Manipulation: A firm has an EBIT of 400000 and interest expenses...
summary: Calculate net income from EBIT by subtracting interest expense and applying the tax rate.
prompt: A firm has an EBIT of $400,000 and interest expenses of $100,000. If the tax rate is 30%, calculate the net income.
solution: Net Income = (EBIT - Interest Expense) × (1 - Tax Rate) = ($400,000 - $100,000) × (1 - 0.30) = $300,000 × 0.70 = $210,000. This metric is crucial for assessing a company's profitability after accounting for all expenses.
